data.title: Why Is the Stock Market Up Today, 3/25/26?
data.description:
Both the S&P 500 (SPX) and the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) are trading higher on Wednesday amid falling oil prices and new developments in the U.S.-Iran war.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Trade QQQ with leverage
  • New trading tool for QQQ bulls/bears
  • Forget margin or options. Here's how the pros trade QQQ

Both sides have outlined proposals to end the war, though key differences still remain. A senior Iranian official noted that the country's first reaction to the terms was "not positive," while a top U.S. official characterized Iran's demands as "ridiculous and unrealistic." Still, investors view these actions as a first step in securing a ceasefire.

Iran Sets Ceasefire Conditions, Vows Strikes Until They are Met

Iran laid out five key conditions to end the conflict and said that it would continue missile and drone strikes until they are met. These include war reparations, recognition of its sovereignty over the Strait of Hormuz, and a full stop to "aggression and assassinations" by the U.S. and Israel.

Meanwhile, senior Iranian officials denied President Trump's claim of direct peace talks between the two sides and accused him of manipulating the oil and stock markets. Earlier this week, Trump called off an attack on the country's power plants for five days to allow for negotiations.
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: New Analyst Coverage Sends Figma (FIG) Stock Lower
data.description:
Shares of Figma (FIG) are slightly down at the time of writing after Oppenheimer (OPY) started covering the stock with a Hold rating. The investment firm sees Figma as a leader in digital design software, with a strong product and clear value for customers. In fact, four-star analyst Brian Schwartz pointed to its fast growth, which has already reached a $1.22 billion revenue run rate. In addition, its web-based platform already includes AI features and is widely used by large companies for design, collaboration, and project management. This helps it stand out in the market.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

That said, there are also worries about how AI could affect Figma going forward. While the company has built a strong position through its technology, Schwartz believes that the shift toward AI could slow down customer growth and reduce how much clients spend. More importantly, newer AI-driven tools and "agentic" automation could lower the need for traditional design platforms, while also increasing competition from AI-first companies.

As a result, Figma's competitive edge could become weaker over time. Nevertheless, the overall outlook is not clearly negative. Schwartz estimates that Figma's total market opportunity is about $33 billion, with only a few competitors at its scale, which supports its long-term potential. However, the stock is already trading at a high valuation, around 9 times forward revenue, suggesting that strong expectations are already priced in.

Is FIG Stock a Good Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Moderate Buy consensus rating on FIG stock based on three Buys, seven Holds, and zero Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average FIG price target of $40.25 per share implies 86.8% upside potential.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: Palantir Technologies Stock (PLTR) Jumps on Analyst Praise for Golden Dome Inclusion
data.description:
Palantir Technologies (PLTR) stock rallied on Wednesday alongside updated analyst coverage. Rosenblatt Securities analyst John McPeake reiterated a Buy rating for PLTR stock and a $200 price target, suggesting a 25.7% upside for the shares. The analysts reaffirmed his bullish coverage of Palantir Technologies in light of the big data company joining President Donald Trump's Golden Dome project.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

President Trump's Golden Dome project has an estimated cost of $185 billion and seeks to create an anti-missile shield for the U.S. This would be similar to the Iron Dome used by Israel to protect it from missile strikes. However, the key difference is that Trump wants the Golden Dome to be space-based. This aligns with the creation of the U.S. Space Force (USSF) during President Trump's previous term.

McPeake argued that the Golden Dome project could be a major win for Palantir Technologies. He claimed it could generate "many billions of dollars" during its first phase. The analyst also said the project is supportive of upsides for PLTR beyond Wall Street's estimates through 2028. Palantir Technologies' role in the project would see it provide data to the Department of War via its Maven Smart System.

Palantir Technologies Stock Movement Today

Palantir Technologies stock was up 2.8% on Wednesday, but was still down 10.49% year-to-date. Despite that drop, the shares have rallied 67.73% over the past 12 months.

PLTR stock trading activity today saw some 12 million shares change hands, compared to a three-month daily average of about 49.56 million shares.
data.author: William White

data.title: Can't Buy SpaceX Yet? These 3 Space Stocks Are the Next Best Thing
data.description:
The space economy is heating up—and all eyes are on Elon Musk's SpaceX. According to a recent report from The Information, SpaceX may file its IPO prospectus as early as this week or next. If it goes public, it won't just mark a major milestone for SpaceX—it could spark fresh momentum across the entire space sector. Amid this buzz, three space stocks—Rocket Lab (RKLB), Intuitive Machines (LUNR), and Planet Labs (PL)—are gaining solid investor attention. Following the report, RKLB stock jumped 10.46%, LUNR surged 18.89%, and PL gained 8.85% at the market open—signaling rising excitement ahead of the potential IPO.

data.title: Oil at $150 Will Trigger a 'Steep Recession,' Warns BlackRock CEO
data.description:
BlackRock (BLK) CEO and co-founder Larry Fink has warned that oil rising to $150 could lead to a "stark and steep recession." Brent crude futures (BZ), the international benchmark for oil, have surged 26% to $96 since the start of the U.S.-Iran war on February 28.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Trade QQQ with leverage
  • New trading tool for QQQ bulls/bears
  • Forget margin or options. Here's how the pros trade QQQ

Fink noted that it is too early to determine the outcome of the war, although he expects one to two scenarios to unfold. The first is for Iran to be integrated into the international community following the end of the war, which would send oil below its pre-war level.

Strait of Hormuz Tensions Keep Oil Elevated

If the first scenario doesn't play out, the second scenario is much less rosy, with Fink calling for a recession and "years of above $100, closer to $150 oil, which has profound implications in the economy."

While traffic through the Strait of Hormuz has slightly picked up in recent days, it's still well below the rate seen before the start of the war. Iran has continued to reiterate that it will attack any U.S.-linked vessel crossing through the passageway and rejected an initial U.S. proposal to end the war, citing the demands as "excessive."
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Dell (DELL) Hasn't Given Up on The Dream of AI Personal Computers. Its Stock Is Surging
data.description:
The stock of Dell Technologies (DELL) is up 4% after the company announced that it is launching a new line of artificial intelligence-enabled (AI) personal computers.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Dell, which also makes AI servers for use in data centers, has introduced a new portfolio of AI computers that includes Dell Pro notebooks, Dell Pro Precision workstations, desktops, monitors and peripherals. The new Dell Pro notebook lineup includes models powered by Intel Core Ultra Series 3 and AMD (AMD) Ryzen AI 400 processors.

The computers support on-device AI workloads and run Microsoft's (MSFT) Copilot+ AI assistant. The new lineup of computers come as Dell and other companies look to further integrate and monetize AI technologies. It's hoped that the addition of AI will boost sales in the market for personal computers.

Dell's AI Strategy

While Dell's AI servers are experiencing strong demand, the company's PC sales have slumped since the Covid-19 pandemic ended. Dell is betting that AI features will help to boost sales moving forward.

Dell Pro Premium targets executives with a 14-inch laptop featuring magnesium alloy chassis and optional Tandem OLED display. The Dell Pro 7 series offers 13- and 14-inch commercial laptops and 2-in-1s that are up to 18% thinner than previous models.

Dell Pro 5 systems provide configuration options across Intel and AMD processors in 14- and 16-inch sizes, while Dell Pro 3 models start at 2.89 pounds and target administrative tasks. The company also introduced the Dell Pro 5 Micro desktop, which Dell describes as its first Copilot+ PC mainstream desktop with 50 TOPS NPU capability.

The Dell Pro Precision workstation line returns with the Dell Pro Precision 5S starting at 3.1 pounds. Higher-end Dell Pro Precision 5 and 7 series mobile workstations will feature NVIDIA RTX PRO Blackwell Generation laptop GPUs, while new Dell Pro Precision 9 desktop models can accommodate up to five NVIDIA RTX PRO Blackwell desktop GPUs.

New Dell Pro P monitors include the 34-inch USB-C Hub Conferencing Monitor with Microsoft Teams and Zoom certification, featuring a Sony Starvis 5MP HDR camera and AI noise cancellation microphones. Additional peripheral products include fingerprint-enabled mice and keyboard combinations.

Pricing was not disclosed in the announcement. Availability varies by product, with some models available March 31 and others scheduled for May release dates.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: GameStop's Q4 Earnings Prove the Rise of PC Gaming Is Bad for GME Stock
data.description:
GameStop (GME) stock could be in trouble soon, as a shift in gaming trends could leave the video game retailer with a shrinking customer base. The company reported its Q4 results after markets closed on Tuesday, and revenue for the holiday quarter fell 10.57% year-over-year to $1.1 billion. A major reason for this revenue drop was the game industry's shift away from physical games.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

PC gaming has largely been digital for over a decade now, as platforms such as Valve's Steam and the Epic Games Store offer direct digital sales to consumers. On top of that, PC gaming is growing. Some experts predict that PC gaming revenue will surpass console gaming revenue as early as 2028. This shift would further harm physical video game retailers, such as GameStop.

Finally, console gaming has also started to turn away from physical games. Instead, video game publishers have pushed for digital sales, which return bigger profits as no physical media has to be created. Adding to that trend are major companies offering subscription services, including Microsoft's (MSFT) Xbox Game Pass, Sony's (SONY) PlayStation Plus, and Nintendo's (NTDOF) Switch Online services. All of these offer games to subscribers without the need for a physical sale.

What Does This Mean for GameStop Stock?

If the shift to digital gaming continues to gain momentum, it would mean ongoing drops in revenue from physical game sales. This could spell trouble for GameStop, as video game sales make up a large portion of its revenue.

This isn't to say that GameStop is unaware of the issue. The retailer has been expanding into other avenues of pop culture to help prop up its sales. That includes buying and selling individual trading cards, such as Pokémon, Dragon Ball Super, Disney (DIS) Lorcana, Hasbro's (HAS) Magic: The Gathering, and sports cards. However, it only deals in graded cards, which limits its audience to collectors and not those who play trading card games.

Is GameStop Stock a Buy, Sell, or Hold?

Turning to Wall Street, traditional analyst coverage of GameStop is lacking. Fortunately, TipRanks' AI analyst Spark has it covered. Spark rates GME stock as Neutral with a $23.50 price target. It cites "improving profitability and cash flow, supported by disciplined cost control, but tempered by ongoing revenue declines and a more risk-prone balance-sheet profile" as reasons for this stance.
data.author: William White

data.title: Cipher Digital Stock (CIFR) Explodes 9% as New Data Center Deal Fuels AI Ambitions
data.description:
Cipher Digital (CIFR) (formerly known as Cipher Mining) saw its share price explode 9% today, following the announcement of a massive new partnership. The former crypto mining firm is moving fast into the world of artificial intelligence after signing a 15-year lease for a new data center. This agreement with an investment-grade hyperscale tenant shows that the company is successfully shifting its focus toward AI computing.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Trade AMZN with leverage
  • New trading tool for AMZN bulls/bears
  • Forget margin or options. Here's how the pros trade AMZN

Long-Term Lease Supports Cipher's Shift to AI

This new agreement marks the third major data center campus for Cipher. The company will build a high-power computing facility at one of its current sites for the unnamed tenant under the 15-year deal. This move follows the recent name change from "Cipher Mining" to "Cipher Digital," which showed its plan to leave the unpredictable world of Bitcoin mining behind.

CEO Tyler Page noted that this third large campus makes the company a trusted partner for the world's leading tech firms. Page stated, "This agreement for our third large AI campus reinforces Cipher's position as a trusted partner to develop high-quality HPC data center infrastructure for the world's leading companies." Cipher is now focused on building the large-scale sites needed to power the next generation of AI and cloud work.

Cipher's New Financing Creates Room for Rapid Growth

Alongside the new lease, Cipher secured a credit facility of up to $200 million. A group of global banks including Morgan Stanley (MS), Goldman Sachs (GS), and JPMorgan (JPM) is supporting this financing. The deal also includes an option to add another $50 million if the company needs it later.

The company is gaining the cash it needs to fund future growth through this new credit line without selling more stock. CFO Greg Mumford explained, "This transaction marks Cipher's first syndicated revolving credit facility and represents a major step in the evolution of our capital structure." He added that the facility provides "non-dilutive capital with enhanced flexibility as we continue to scale the business."

Cipher's Strategic Changes Bring Stable Income

Cipher is trading unpredictable crypto prices for steady and reliable income as it moves away from Bitcoin. The company has already secured billions in revenue from top tech clients in earlier deals. This includes a major $5.5 billion agreement with Amazon Web Services (AWS) (AMZN) and a separate project backed by Google (GOOGL).

This change is attracting a new wave of large investors who want to be part of the AI infrastructure world. Analysts believe that the stock could see even more gains later this year as Cipher finishes building these massive campuses.

Is Cipher Mining Stock a Good Buy?

Turning to TipRanks, Wall Street has a Strong Buy consensus rating on Cipher Digital stock (CIFR) based on nine unanimous Buy ratings. The average 12-month CIFR stock price target of $24.13 indicates 50.81% upside potential.
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: Trump Launches Tech Panel with Nvidia, Meta, and Google CEOs On Board
data.description:
President Trump announced the creation of the President's Council of Advisors on Science and Technology (PCAST) through an executive order on Wednesday. The group will advise Trump on technology and AI developments and will include the CEOs of Nvidia (NVDA), Meta Platforms (META), and Google (GOOGL).

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Trade QQQ with leverage
  • New trading tool for QQQ bulls/bears
  • Forget margin or options. Here's how the pros trade QQQ

"The United States has the opportunity to lead the world in AI," said Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg in a statement to the Wall Street Journal. "I'm honored to join the President's council and work with other industry leaders to help make this happen."

PCAST to Advise on AI, Quantum Computing, and Biotech

The panel will be co-chaired by White House technology advisers David Sacks and Michael Kratsios and will be terminated in two years unless renewed by Trump.

Previous presidents, including George W. Bush, Barack Obama and Joe Biden, have created similar groups to address innovations in technology and to remain globally competitive. Outside of AI, PCAST will also focus on quantum computing, advanced biotechnology, and soliciting information from stakeholders.
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Microsoft (MSFT) Slides on AI Spending and OpenAI Risk, but Analysts See Up to 65% Upside
data.description:
Microsoft (MSFT) is under pressure as investors reassess its AI strategy, even as Wall Street analysts remain broadly positive on the stock. The shares are down about 23% this year and more than 30% from their recent peak. The move reflects a shift in focus from AI growth potential to the cost and pace of returns.
